txt: Messinger, Arthur (or Massinger), B.A. from St. Alban Hall, sup. 7 Dec., 1571, fellow of Merton Coll. 1572, M.A. 25 June, 1577, incorporated at Cambridge 1578, M.P. Weymouth and Melcombe Regis 1588-9, 1593, Shaftesbury 1601. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary & O.H.S. iv. 270.

Marston, Vincent (Merston), B.A. from St. Alban Hall 16 Feb., 1570-1, fellow of Exeter Coll. 1572-77, M.A. 1 July, 1573, rector of Lezant, Cornwall, 1577, and canon of Exeter 1577, rector of Lanreath, Cornwall, 1583. See Boase, 46; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Norrys, John, B.A. from St. Alban Hall 21 Feb., 1570-1, fellow of Merton Coll. 1572-86, M.A. 25 June, 1577, licenced to practice medicine 1 July, 1587, B.Med. supd. July, 1587. See Foster's Index Eccl. & O.H.S. iv. 270.

Norris, Edward, of St. Alban Hall in and before 1572; student of Christ Church 1572, B.A. 19 Feb., 1573-4, M.A. 22 May, 1577, vicar of Tetbury, co. Gloucester, 1578; perhaps father of the next. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Limiter, Stephen, of St. Alban Hall, in and before 1572, fellow of All Souls' Coll. 1572, B.A. 17 June, 1573, M.A. 17 May, 1577, sup. for licence to preach 7 May, 1583; incorporated at Cambridge 1583.

Jessop, William (Jesoppe), of St. Alban Hall in or before 1572; scholar Trinity Coll. 1573, B.A. 17 May, 1576, M.A. from St. John's Coll., Cambridge, 1589; one of these names a student of Middle Temple 1579 (as son and heir of Richard, of Broom Hall, co. Worcester, gent.); another prebendary of Fontmell in Shaftesbury 1582, rector of Long Crichell 1587, and of Cheselborne, Dorset, 1588. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg. & Foster's Index Eccl.

Weston, Hugh, B.A. from St. Alban Hall 19 Feb., 1570-1; fellow Lincoln Coll. 1573-7, M.A. 6 July, 1577 ("suspected to be a papist"); perhaps rector of Carlton-Curliew 1576, and of Narborough, (both) co. Leicester, 1592. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Goodman, Adam, of St. Alban Hall, in or before 1572; fellow Brasenose Coll. 1573, B.A. 15 Feb., 1576-7.

Hollande, John, of co. Denbigh, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. entry under date 1575, aged 19; B.A. 18 Feb., 1577-8, M.A. 3 July, 1581, rector of Llanwyddelan, co. Montgomery, 1581, and sinecure rector of Llanfor, co. Merioneth, 1588, vicar of Guilsfield, co. Montgomery, 1586, canon of St. Asaph 1599. See Thomas, 706; & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Lawton, Thomas, of Cheshire, arm. St. Alban Hall, matric. entry under date 1575, aged 17; bar.-at-law, Inner Temple, 1584, bencher 1597, as 3s. John, of Lawton, Cheshire, gent. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Jenkinson, Zachary, B.A. from St. Alban Hall 10 Feb., 1573-4; fellow of Brasenose Coll. 1575-6; vicar of Stapleford, co. Leicester, 1579-94, and of West Retford, Notts, 1595-1600. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Noone, Andrew, of Northants, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. entry under date 1575, aged 20; B.A. sup. 30 Jan., 1576-7.
Eyton, Thomas, of Flints, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. entry under date 1575, aged 17; B.A. 7 Feb., 1577-8.
Griffith, Rice (Griffyne), of Flints, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. entry under date 1575, aged 19.
Lullay, Robert, of Yorks, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. entry under date 1575, aged 18.
Owe, John, of Wilts, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. entry under date 1575, aged 18.

Crompton, (Sir) Thomas, of London. St. Alban Hall, matric.-entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 19 (as pleb.); B.A. from Merton Coll. 28 Jan., 1578-9, M.A. 1 Dec., 1581, B. & D.C.L. 11 July, 1589, of the Inner Temple 1581 (son of Sir Thomas), judge high court of admiralty 1589-1608, chancellor, of the diocese of London 1607-11, master in chancery 1608-9, vicar-general to archbishop of Canterbury 1611, M.P. Boroughbridge 1597-8, Whitchurch 1601, Oxford University 1604-9, knighted 23 July, 1603, died in 1608-9. See Foster's Parliamentary Dic.

Finmor, William (Fynmer), of Berks, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 16; fellow of St. John's Coll. 1578, from Reading, B.A. 4 July, 1582, B.C.L. 5 Nov., 1588 (son of William, mayor of Reading 1577, 1586), buried at Ferry Hinksey 1646, grandfather of William next-named, and of Richard and Thomas last-named. See Memorials of the Family of Fynmore by W. P. W. Phillimore.

Fulbeck, William, of co. Lincoln, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 17; scholar Corpus Christi Coll. 1580, B.A. 25 Oct., 1581, fellow 1582, M.A. 30 May, 1584, bar.-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1591, took orders, vicar of Waldeshare, Kent, 1603. See Foster's Judges and Barristers; Ath. i. 726; & D.N.B.

Wood, William, of co. Anglesea, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 19; fellow All Souls' Coll. 1577, B.C.L. 29 May, 1583, D.C.L. 17 June, 1587; advocate of Doctors' Commons 1590; died in 1605, buried at Tallin, isle of Anglesey. See Coote's Civilians, 61; & Fasti, i. 240.

Dutton, Thomas, of Cheshire, 'minister Verbi.' St. Alban Hall, matric.-entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 24 (? 34); perhaps rector of Barlborough, co. Derby, 1585, vicar of Frodsham, Cheshire, 1589. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Bruen, John, of Cheshire, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric.-entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 18; 'puritan layman' (son of John, of Bruen Stapleford, Cheshire, died 1625. See Dictionary of National Biography.
Cape, James, of Somerset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric.-entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 19, B.A. 7 Feb., 1577-8, vicar of Herne, Kent, 1616. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Crowther, Anthony (or Growther), of Yorks, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric.-entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 17, B.A. (sup. 14 May) 1582; M.A. from University Coll. 2 July, 1588.
Wight, Daniel, of co. Warwick, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 16; B.A. from St. John's Coll. 4 July, 1582, as White; father of Nathan 1626.
Bodley, William (Bodleygh), of London, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric.-entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 19 (? a student of Gray's Inn 1580). See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Moon, Morgan, of Dorset, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 26; B.A. sup. 21 April, 1578. See O.H.S. xii. 77.
Bruene, Thomas, of Cheshire, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric.-entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 15. See Ormerod's Cheshire, ii. 309, 322.
Clatworth, William, of Devon, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric.-entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 20; B.A. March, 1579-80.
Brock, Francis, of (? Jersey), pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric.-entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 19; B.A. 9 July, 1579.
Berkeley, Anthony (Barklay), of co. Gloucester, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric.-entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 12.
Brerewod, John (Birewood), of Cheshire, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric.-entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 17.
Williams, Percival, of co. Somerset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 15.
Millet, Thomas, (of Guernsey ?), pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 23.
Marshall, (—), of Devon, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 17.
Moon, Robert, of Dorset, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 19.
Park, William, of Notts, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 19.
Palmer, Bartholomew, of co. Leicester, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. entry under date circa 1578, aged 25.

Dugdale, Thomas, of co. Lancaster, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric.-entry under date 28 Feb., 1580-1, aged 22; B.A. from Trinity Coll. 22 Feb., 1586-7, rector of Tarrant Hinton, Dorset, 1585, probably father of the last named. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus & John 1583.

Anthonio, Anthony de, of London, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric.-entry under date 28 Feb., 1580-1, aged 17; fellow of Merton Coll. 1580, B.A. 4 June, 1580, M.A. 9 July, 1585, the matric.-entry is evidently post dated. See O.H.S., x. 377.

Corderoy, Jerome (Cordroye), of Wilts, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric.-entry under date 28 Feb., 1580-1, aged 18, B.A. 7 July, 1581; M.A. from Hart Hall 3 June, 1584, chaplain Merton College 1590. See Ath., ii. 47; & D.N.B.

White, Bartholomew (Whyte), of Dorset, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. entry under date 28 Feb., 1580-1, aged 12; student of Middle Temple 1583, as of Fittleford, Dorset, esq. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Broadgate, Richard (Brodegate), of co. Leicester, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric.-entry under date 28 Feb., 1580-1, aged 17.
Mainwaring, Edmund (Manwaring), of Cheshire, arm. St. Alban Hall, matric. entry under date 28 Feb., 1580-1, aged 16.
Whorwood, Thomas (Hawywoode), of Kent, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. entry under date 28 Feb., 1580-1, aged 19.
Barber, Sylvester, of Rutland, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric.-entry under date 28 Feb., 1580-1, aged 27.
Lemprier, Hugh, of (? Jersey), gen. St. Alban Hall, matric. entry under date 28 Feb., 1580-1, aged 16.
Scadden, Nicholas, of Devon, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. entry under date 28 Feb., 1580-1, aged 29.
Lower, Edward, of Somerset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. entry under date 28 Feb., 1580-1, aged 16.
Crosse, John, of Dorset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric.-entry under date 28 Feb., 1580-1, aged 22.

Moone, Maximilian, of Dorset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 16; of Fleet, Dorset, student of Middle Temple 1584 (1s. Robert, of Baunton, or Bothenhampton, Dorset); died 14 Oct., 1612, aged 48; brother of John 1586; father of Maximilian Mohun 1613, see page 1018. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Salusbury, Henry, of co. Denbigh, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 15 Dec., 1581, aged 20; B.A. 1 Feb., 1584-5, M.A. 28 June, 1588 (as Robert); of the family of Dolbelider, author of "Dictionarium Britannicum." See Ath. i. 589; & Fasti, i. 225.

Perkins, John, of co. Gloucester, pleb: St. Alban Hall, matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 16; B.A. 27 April, 1586, M.A. 20 May, 1591, student of Gray's Inn 1591, as of Upleadon, co. Gloucester. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Waters, James, of Sussex, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 17, B.A. 22 June, 1583, M.A. 12 May, 1597; vicar of Bishops Lavington, Wilts, 1592. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Denshier, Jeffery, of co. Leicester, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 19; called Devonshire in Mat. Reg., administration at Oxford (granted) 2 Nov., 1604.
Hulson, Thomas, of London, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 18; one of these names rector of Great Horkesly, Essex, 1553-9. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Reynolds, Lionel (Raynolds), of Dorset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 9 Feb., 1581-2, aged 19, B.A. 19 Feb., 1583-4; M.A. from University Coll. 5 July, 1594.
Fort, John, of Somerset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 2 March, 1581-2, aged 18; a student of Lincoln's Inn 1584. See Foster's Inns of Court Register.
Hall, John (Haule), of Oxon, "famulus Mri Case," matric. 2 Aug., 1581, aged 20; "scholaris Mri Case"; B.A. from St. Alban Hall 30 April, 1583.
Keate, Thomas, of Dorset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 15; and again 14 Dec., 1582, aged 16, from Merton Coll.
Worswick, Thomas, of co. Stafford, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 17; B.A. from Merton Coll. 15 June, 1585.
Norman, Thomas, of Dorset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 19; B.A. 6 July, 1583. M.A. 11 June, 1586.
Valentyne, Thomas, of Somerset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 21, B.A. 7 Dec., 1582.
Eglesfild, Henry, of London, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 17.
Prater, Richard, of Berks, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 17.
Fox, Edward, of co. York, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 1 Dec., 1581, aged 19.

Trafford, Richard, of London, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 18 May, 1582, aged 16, B.A. supd. 17 Dec., 1584; fellow Merton Coll. 1586, M.A. 4 July, 1590, proctor 1597, B. and D.C.L. supd. 7 June, 1603, allowed 4 terms at Cambridge. See O.H.S. iv. 274.
Weedon, Robert, of Bucks, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 12 Oct., 1582, aged 16; student of Middle Temple 1587, as 2nd son of Richard, of Pedner, Bucks, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Hallet, John, of Dorset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 15 March, 1582-3, aged 18; B.A. 11 June, 1586, M.A. 26 March, 1602, rector of Pendomer, Somerset, 1590. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Porret, William, of co. Durham, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 1 March, 1582-3, aged 19; B.A. 29 Feb., 1583-4, M.A. 13 June, 1586.
Punford, George, of Dorset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 1 March, 1582-3, aged 26.
Slade, John, of Somerset, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 1 March, 1582-3, aged 18.
Weedon, William, of Bucks, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 19 Oct., 1582, aged 18.

Daye, John, of London, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 11 Oct., 1583, aged 17, B.A. 15 Feb., 1586-7; fellow Oriel Coll., 1588-1622, M.A. 30 Nov., 1591, licenced to preach 16 March, 1604-5, B.D. 27 June, 1611 (son of John, of London, printer), vicar of St. Mary, Oxford, 1608-22, rector of Little Thurlow, Suffolk, 1622, until his death in 1637, brother of Lionel, 1587. See Ath., ii. 412; Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus; & D.N.B.

Mytton, (Sir) Peter (Mutton), of Flints, gen. fil. St. Alban Hall, matric. 18 Oct., 1583, aged 18 "Mr. Case's scholar"; of Lannerch (s. John), bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1594, bencher 1622, a master in chancery 1624-38, chief justice of North Wales, and knighted 5 June, 1622, M.P. co. Denbigh, 1604-11, and Carnarvon 1624-5. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Warham, Edward (Warum), of Dorset, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 11 Oct., 1583, aged 18, B.A. 4 Dec., 1587, M.A. 10 July, 1590; student of Inner Temple 1599 (as of Osmington, Dorset, gent.); perhaps rector of West Lydford 1592, and of East Lydford, Somerset, 1613; brother of John 1584. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.; & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Clifton, William (Clyfton), of Somerset, eq. aur. fil. St. Alban Hall, matric. 18 March, 1583-4, aged 10; of the Middle Temple 1591, as 4th son of John, of Barrington, Somerset, knight; brother of Gervase, Amias, and John 1586. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Warham, John, of Dorset, gent. St. Alban Hall., matric. 20 March, 1583-4, aged 20; student of Middle Temple 1595, as son and heir of Edward, of Burton, Dorset, gent.; brother of Edward 1583. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Whinnell, Thomas, of Somerset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 12 April, 1583, aged 21; rector of Askerswell, Dorset, 1594; father of John and of the next-named. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Nevile, William (Nevyle), of Notts, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 15 Nov., 1583, aged 15; son of John, of Grove, Notts, and brother of George 1583.
Harris, Henry, of Dorset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 11 Oct., 1583, aged 15; B.A. 15 June, 1588, dispensed towards M.A. 14 March, 1589-90.
Nevyll, George, of Notts, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 15 Nov., 1583, aged 14; son of John, of Grove, Notts, and brother of William.
Lloyd, Robert, of co. Denbigh, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 12 April, 1583, aged 16; B.A. 4 April, 1587, M.A. 3 June, 1590.
Nettleship, William (Netelshippe), of Notts, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 12 April, 1583, aged 18.
Drury, Richard (Drewrye), of Berks, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 12 April, 1583, aged 18.
Warham, Richard (Warum), of Dorset, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 11 Oct., 1583, aged 16.
Hughes, David, of co. Carnarvon, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 12 April, 1583, aged 17.
Owens, Morgan, of co. Carnarvon, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 12 April, 1583, aged 18.
Chapman, Richard, of Dorset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 20 March, 1583-4, aged 17.
Owens, Edward, of co. Denbigh, arm. St. Alban Hall, matric. 12 April, 1583, aged 18.
Standon, William, of Devon, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 24 Jan., 1583-4, aged 26.
White, John, of Dorset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. entry 11 Oct., 1583, aged 14.
Gannet, Robert, of Dorset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 12 April, 1583, aged 20.
Mayo, John, of Dorset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 3 May, 1583, aged 18.

Helme, William, of Wilts, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 10 July, 1584, aged 19; fellow Exeter Coll. 1587-1615, B.A. 9 July, 1590, M.A. 10 May, 1593, B.D. 23 March, 1603-4; rector of Evendale, co. Worcester, 1610, vicar of Bishopstone, Wilts, 1613-39; died 1639. See Boase, 49; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Marshall, Eustace, B.A. from St. Alban Hall 26 Jan., 1580-1, M.A. 2 July, 1583, fellow Exeter Coll. 1584-6, rector of Langtree, Devon, 1585. See Boase, 49; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Carew, John, of co. Cambridge, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 29 March, 1584, aged 21; of the Inner Temple 1587, as of Chesterton, co. Cambridge. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Widley, George, of Berks, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 5 March, 1584-5, aged 18, B.A. 25 Feb., 1586-7, M.A. 1 June, 1590, minister of God's word at Dartmouth. See Ath. i. 753.
Adyn, Robert, of Dorset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 5 March, 1584-5, aged 13 (? B.A. from New Coll. 27 April, 1598).
Furnas, Mark (Furnis), of co. Derby, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 26 Feb., 1584-5, aged 20.
Browne, John, of Wilts, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 5 March, 1584-5, aged 17.

Clifton, Amias (Ames Clyfton), of Somerset, eq. aur. fil. St. Alban Hall, matric. 18 March, 1585-6, aged 14; of Gray's Inn 7th and migrated to Lincoln's Inn 27 Feb., 1588-9, as 2nd son of Sir John, brother of Gervase and John same date, and of William 1584. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Clifton, (Sir) Gervase, of Somerset, eq. aur. fil. St. Alban Hall, matric. 18 March, 1585-6, aged 16 (eldest son of Sir John, of Brimpton, Somerset), M.P. Hunts 1597 and 1601, Lord Clifton of Layton Bromswold, Hunts, by writ 9 July, 1608, died in Oct., 1618, brother of John same date.

Clifton, John, of Somerset, eq. aur. fil. St. Alban Hall, matric. 18 March, 1585-6, aged 13; of the Middle Temple 1591, as 3rd son of John, of Barrington, Somerset, knight; brother of Gervase and Amias same date. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Slade, Matthew, of Devon, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 29 Oct., 1585, aged 18, B.A. 13 Jan., 1588-9; (2s. John), taught school in Devonshire, became rector of the academy or gymnasium at Amsterdam. See Ath. ii. 154.

Clutterbuck, Adrian (Clitterbooke), of Berks, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 15 Oct., 1585, aged 16; of the Inner Temple 1593, as of Remenham, Berks, late of Clement's Inn. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Plum(p)tree, Alban, of Beds, cler. fil. St. Alban Hall, matric. 23 April, 1585, aged 19, B.A. 15 Feb., 1586-7; perhaps son of Alban, rector of Whipsnade, Beds, 1561. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Clay, John, of Yorks, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 23 April, 1585, aged 18; of the Middle Temple 1586, as son and heir of John, of Clayhouse, Yorks, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Orde, Richard, of Northumberland, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 2 July, 1585, aged 18; bar.-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1600, as of Horsley, Northumberland. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Berjew, Richard (Beriew), of Dorset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 2 July, 1585, aged 20; B.A. 15 June, 1588, rector of Litton-Cheney, Dorset, 1594. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Wolfe, Robert (Woulfe), of Northants, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 23 April, 1585, aged 18, B.A. 7 Feb., 1588-9; M.A. from Corpus Christi Coll. 6 Dec., 1593.
Greene, William (Grene or Greve), of co. Leicester, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 29 Oct., 1585, aged 19; B.A. 16 Dec., 1589.
Wilcock, Thomas, of Kent, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 29 Oct., 1585, aged 18, B.A. from Merton Coll. 20 Feb., 1589-90.
Bragg, Edward, of Somerset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 18 March, 1585-6, aged 24.
Wilse, John, of co. Gloucester, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 2 July, 1585, aged 23.
Hodges, John, of Somerset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 18 March, 1585-6, aged 16.
Hunte, Leonard, of Devon, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 18 March, 1585-6, aged 16.
Bateman, Stephen, of Kent, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 29 Oct., 1585, aged 16.

Moone, John, of Dorset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 8 July, 1586, aged 18; of Mangerton, Dorset, student of Middle Temple 1591 (as 3rd son of Robert, of Bothenhampton, Dorset, gent.); brother of Maximilian 1581. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Hiron, Robert, of co. Lincoln, cler. fil. St. Alban Hall, matric. 4 Nov., 1586, aged 18; B.A. from Brasenose Coll. 9 July, 1590, M.A. 4 July, 1594 (as Hieron); admon. at Oxford 19 July, 1603.
Sturdie, Ralph, of Richmondshire, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 4 Nov., 1586, aged 17, B.A. 16 Oct., 1590; student of Gray's Inn 1591, as of Yafford, Yorks. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Stoodlaie, Thomas, of Dorset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 8 July, 1586, aged 17; one of these names rector of Portland, Dorset, 1598. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Cooper, John, of co. Worcester, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 17 Feb., 1586-7, aged 21; B.A. from Magdalen Coll. 30 Oct., 1591.
Mills, Anthony (Milles), of Wilts, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 17 Feb., 1586-7, aged 16; B.A. from New Coll. 17 May, 1595.
Martin, Thomas, of Dorset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 8 July, 1586, aged 16; called Merton in Mat. Reg.
Smith, William (Smyth), of Somerset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 8 July, 1586, aged 16.
Compton, Henry, of Somerset, arm. St. Alban Hall, matric. 17 Feb., 1586-7, aged 16.
Martin, Richard, of Dorset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 8 July, 1586, aged 14.
Allard, Francis, of Kent, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 8 July, 1586, aged 13.

Day, Lionel, of London, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 8 Dec., 1587, aged 17; B.A. from Oriel Coll. 5 Feb., 1590-1, fellow Balliol Coll., M.A. 6 Feb., 1593-4, B.D. 1 June, 1608, licenced to preach 26 Oct., 1609, rector of Whichford, co. Warwick, 1608, died 1640, buried in chancel, brother of John 1583, and father of the next named. See Fasti, i. 326.
Clowes, William, of co. Derby, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 21 April, 1587, aged 18; B.A. 25 Oct., 1590, M.A. 30 June, 1595.
Hodgins, Roger, of Oxon, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 28 April, 1587, aged 15; called "Hodgkins" in Mat. Reg.
Fitzrandulph, Edward, of co. Derby, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 19 June, 1587, aged 10.
Gregorye, William, of Northants, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 12 July, 1587, aged 24.
Virgin, John, of Somerset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 7 April, 1587, aged 22.

Greene, Francis, of Northants, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 3 May, 1588, aged 16; B.A. 6 July, 1592; one of these names vicar of Neston, Cheshire, 1605, which he resigned in 1643, being "disabled by age from officiating." See Foster's Index Eccl. & Add. MS. 15,671, p. 219.
Parris, William, of Dorset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 20 March, 1589-90, aged 24; for one of these names see Cooper, ii. 529; & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Blounte, William, of co. Stafford, 'Gen. Cond.' St. Alban Hall, matric. 17 Oct., 1589, aged 26.

Harcourt, Robert (Harecourt), of co. Stafford, arm. St. Alban Hall, matric. 10 April, 1590, aged 15; student of Middle Temple 1593 (as son and heir of Walter, of Stanton Harcourt, Oxon, kt.), had a grant of letters patent for Guiana from James I.; died 20 May, 1631, aged 57; buried at Stanton Harcourt. See Ath. ii. 143; D.N.B.; & Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Brydges, Giles (Brugges), of co. Gloucester, arm. fil. St. Alban Hall, matric. 27 Nov., 1590, aged 17; possibly son of Charles, of Wilton Castle, co. Hereford, and if so created a baronet 17 May, 1627, M.P. Tewkesbury 1621-2, co. Hereford 1625-9, died in Aug., 1637, father of John 1638. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.
Rice, John, of Flints, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 15 Oct., 1590, aged 18; B.A. from Christ Church 9 Feb., 1593-4; M.A. from All Souls' Coll. 18 Jan., 1597-8.
Ferman, Thomas, of co. Gloucester, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 29 Jan., 1590-1, aged 16; called Farmer in Mat. Reg.
Lloyd, William, of Northants, cler. fil. St. Alban Hall, matric. 30 Oct., 1590, aged 14, subs. as Floyd.
Floyde, William, of Northants, cler. fil. St. Alban Hall, matric. 30 Oct., 1590, aged 14, as Lloyd.
Langston, John, of co. Worcester, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 29 Jan., 1590-1, aged 17.
Hawkins, John, of co. Worcester, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 27 Nov., 1590, aged 14.
Worrall, Thomas, of Cheshire, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 10 April, 1590, aged 20.

Bridges, Richard, of Wilts, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 3 March, 1591-2, aged 14; B.A. 13 Dec., 1595, M.A. 22 March, 1589-9, one of these names rector of Winterbourne, co. Gloucester, 1600, probably father of the next named. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Elks, William, of Wilts, gent. St. Alban Hall., matric. 7 May, 1591, aged 17; B.A. 20 June, 1594, M.A. 16 April, 1597, rector of Enborne, Berks, 1608. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Astill, William, of Northants, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 7 May, 1591, aged 17; B.A. 12 Feb., 1594-5, M.A. 20 May, 1598.
Steare, Ralph, of Yorks, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 23 April, 1591, aged 16; B.A. from Merton Coll. 26 Nov., 1596.
Butcher, Harbert, of Sussex, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 3 March, 1591-2, aged 17.
Ilbery, William, of Berks, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 10 March, 1591-2, aged 33.
Bridges, Edmond, of Wilts, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 3 March, 1591-2, aged 17.
Floyde, Samuel, of Bucks, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 23 July, 1591, aged 31.
Payer, John, of Dorset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 26 March, 1591, aged 19.

Rashley, Thomas, of Devon, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 28 May, 1592, B.A. 19 Jan., 1595-6; student of Middle Temple 1605, as of South Molton, Devon, esq., and secretary to the Marquis of Exeter. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Cooper, Adam (Couper), of London, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 20 Oct., 1592, aged 18; B.A. 9 Feb., 1592-3, studied 3 years and more in logic in Emanuel Coll., Cambridge.
Macklen, John, of Salop, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 20 Oct., 1592, aged 30.

Francklin, James, of Kent, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 23 Nov., 1593, aged 14; B.A. from Merton Coll. 21 June, 1597; perhaps bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1609, treasurer 1634 (as 3rd son of Arthur, of Wye, Kent, gent.). See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Prew, William, of Somerset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 22 March, 1593-4, aged 17; B.A. 17 Oct., 1597, M.A. 15 June, 1602 (then in orders), rector of Hardington, Somerset, 1600-9, and of Ditton, Kent, 1608. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Audley, Richard, of Wilts, arm. St. Alban Hall, matric. 23 Nov., 1593, aged 15; B.A. 2 July, 1597. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Mo(o)re, Edward, of Sussex, arm. St. Alban Hall, matric. entry 23 Nov., 1593, aged 12.

Johnson, Thomas, of Beds, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 3 May, 1594, aged 16 (subs. as Jhonson); B.A. from Oriel Coll. 23 Oct., 1596, M.A. 12 Nov., 1601 (incorporated at Cambridge 1604), sup. for licence to practice medicine 13 June, 1608, B. and D.Med. 26 June, 1609; buried in St. Mary's, Oxford, 16 Nov., 1621; will at Oxford proved 8 Oct., 1623.
Wood, John, of co. Hereford, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 3 May, 1594, aged 20; perhaps B.A. from Merton Coll. 7 June, 1594; M.A. from St. Mary Hall 25 June, 1600.
Brushe, Richard, of Wilts, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 3 May, 1594, aged 13; B.A. from Magdalen Coll. 15 July, 1605.
Hatton, Hugh, of Cheshire, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 3 May, 1594, aged 20; B.A. 13 June, 1594.
Browne, Thomas, of Wilts, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 3 May, 1594, aged 16.
Potts, Thomas, of Beds, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 3 May, 1594, aged 18.

Hakewill, George (Hackewell), of Devon, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 15 May, 1595, aged 16; fellow Exeter Coll. 1596-1611, B.A. 8 July, 1599, M.A. 29 April, 1602, B.D. 27 March, 1610, D.D. 2 July, 1611, rector of his college 1642-9, built the chapel, admitted of Lincoln's Inn in 1614 (3s. John, of Exeter), archdeacon of Surrey 1617, chaplain to Prince Charles, rector of Heanton Punchardon 1611 until his death, buried there 5 April, 1649, aged 72; brother of William 1613, and father of John 1633. See Lansdowne MS. 985, f. 140; Ath. iii. 253; Boase, 53, 67; Burrows, 500; D.N.B.; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Venner, Tobias (Vanner), of Somerset, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 15 May, 1595, aged 18, B.A. 1 Feb., 1598-9, M.A. 7 July, 1603, B. and D.Med. 31 March, 1613, practiced at Bridgwater, and at Bath, where he died 27 March, 1660; father of John 1624, and of Tobias. See Ath. iii. 491.
Fogg, George, of Kent, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 15 May, 1595, aged 18; B.A. 1 Feb., 1598-9, M.A. 10 Nov., 1601; licenced to practice medicine 16 Oct., 1602.
Key, Richard, of London, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 21 Nov., 1595, aged 20; subs. more like "Keyd" perhaps "Keye." See O.H.S. xi. 211.
Willy, Edmund (Willie), of Somerset, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 15 May, 1595, aged 19.
Fogg, William, of Kent, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 14 Oct., 1596, aged 18; perhaps bar.-atlaw, of Gray's Inn, 1607. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Robinson, Anthony, of co. Gloucester, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 14 Oct., 1597, aged 15; student of Middle Temple 1601, (as son and heir of Robert, of Gloucester, gent.), M.P. Gloucester 1621-2, 1624-5, and mayor 1629. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Hall, William, of Kent, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. Nov., 1597, aged 22; student of Middle Temple 1600, as son and heir of William, of Wilsborough, Kent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Glasier, John (Glasyer), of Sussex, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. — Nov., 1597, aged 17; possibly student of Gray's Inn 1604, as of Staple Inn, gent. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Glyd, Thomas, of Sussex, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. Nov., 1597, aged 16; called "Glide" in Mat. Reg.

Culme, Benjamin, (s. Hugh, of Molland), Devon, arm. St. Alban Hall, matric. 10 Nov., 1598, aged 15, B.A. 8 June, 1602; M.A. from Lincoln Coll. 24 April, 1605, rector of Trim 1619, preb. of Malahidert in St. Patrick's 1615, precentor of Kilkenny 1624, rector of Rathmore in diocese of Meath 1624, dean of St. Patrick's, Dublin, 1625, forced thence by the rebellion in 1641, and retired to Mudghill, near Lydiard St. John's, Wilts, where he died 21 Oct., 1657, aged 76. See Cotton's Fasti Eccl. Hib., ii.; & Fasti, i. 305.

Ashburnham, Lawrence, of Sussex, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 10 Nov., 1598, aged 15; of Bromham, Sussex, admitted to Gray's Inn 1602 (son of Adam), married at All Hallows-in-the-Wall 10 Feb., 1617-18, Bridget, eldest dau. of Sir George Fleetwood, of the Vache, Bucks, grandfather of John last named. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Highmore, Edward, of Dorset, cler. fil. St. Alban Hall, matric. 13 May, 1598, aged 19; B.A. 29 Jan., 1601-2, M.A. 5 July, 1605, rector of Caundle Purse, Dorset, 1603, and of Goathill, Somerset, 1603, rector of Winterbourne Stickland, Dorset, 1615; father of the next-named. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Gulson, Nathaniel, of co. Leicester, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 10 Nov., 1598, aged 15; B.A. from Trinity Coll. 30 June, 1603, M.A. 10 July, 1607, created B.D. 31 Aug., 1636, D.D. 8 July, 1637, rector of Lyndon, Rutland, 1617, and of Wymondham, co. Leicester, 1628. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Ashburnham, John, of Sussex, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 10 Nov., 1598, aged 14; admitted to Gray's Inn 1602, as second son of Adam, of Bromham, Sussex, knighted at Titchfield, Hants, 12 Sept., 1625, sometime servant to the Queen of Bohemia. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Johnsonn, Richard, of London, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 23 Feb., 1598-9, aged 16; perhaps B.A. from Magdalen Coll. 29 Jan., 1599-1600.
Westerdale, Christopher, of co. Gloucester, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 5 May, 1598, aged 17.

Trenchard, (Sir) Thomas, of Dorset, equitis fil. St. Alban Hall, matric. 13 July, 1599, aged 16, B.A. 16 April, 1602; of Wolverton, student of Middle Temple 1604 (as 2s. George, of Wolverton, Dorset, kt.), knighted 15 Dec., 1613, M.P. Dorset 1621-2, and Nov., 1645, till 1653, (L.P.) a rumper 1659; brother of John 1602. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Adderley, Humphrey, of co. Warwick, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 12 Oct., 1599, aged 14; admitted to Lincoln's Inn 1601, son of Humphrey Adderley, of Weddington, master of the wardrobe to Henry VIII., Edward VI., Queen Mary, and Queen Elizabeth, died 1 Nov., 1637. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Yarburghe, Robert (Yearburghe), of co. Lincoln, arm. St. Alban Hall, matric. 24 Nov., 1599, aged 17; student of Lincoln's Inn 1601; son of Francis, of Northrop, co. Lincoln. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Winge, John (Wynge), of Oxon, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. entry 12 Oct., 1599, aged 14; B.A. from Queen's Coll. 16 Feb., 1603-4; perhaps rector of Elmdon, co. Warwick, 1634. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Dauntesey, Ralph, of Wilts, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 19 Oct., 1599, aged 16; of Lincoln's Inn 1601. See Ambrose. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Gardiner, Richard (Gardyner), of Surrey, gent, St. Alban Hall, matric. 19 Oct., 1599, aged 18; B.A. from All Souls' Coll. 3 Dec., 1599.
Danvers, John, of Wilts, arm. St. Alban Hall, matric. 29 Feb., 1599-1600, aged 17; B.A. from Jesus Coll. 11 Dec., 1605.
Glover, Benjamin, of Kent, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 11 May, 1599, aged 24; called "Lover" in Mat. Reg.
Jenkinson, Robert, of co. Lincoln, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 24 Nov., 1599, aged 17.
Jones, Evan (Joanes), of Salop, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 12 Oct., 1599, aged 16.
Boughton, Edward, of Essex, arm. St. Alban Hall, matric. 2 Nov., 1599, aged 16.
Chafin, Thomas, of Wilts, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 19 Oct., 1599, aged 18.

Bayly, Thomas, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 31 Oct., 1600, aged 18; demy Magdalen Coll. 160210, B.A. 12 Dec., 1604, M.A. 8 July, 1607, fellow 1610-15, B.D. 11 May, 1621, licenced to preach 18 May, 1621, rector of Manningford Bruce, Wilts, 1621, one of the assembly of divines at Westminster, rector of Beckenham, Kent, in 1647, and of Mildenhall, Wilts, from where he was ejected, 1660, father of William 1636, buried at Marlborough 27 March, 1663. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus; Calamy, iii. 367; D.N.B.; Bloxam, v. 5; Ath., iii. 633; & Add. MS., 15,671.

Rogers, George, of Kent, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 12 Dec., 1600, aged 17; B.A. 15 June, 1604, M.A. 13 May, 1607; perhaps D.Med. Padua 17 Dec., 1612, incorporated at Oxford 4 July, 1614, (2s. Francis, of Dartford, Kent), fellow college of physicians 1616; buried at St. Dunstan's-in-the-West 18 Nov., 1622; see Munk's Roll, i. 163, where he states that this fellow graduated from Catherine Hall, Cambridge; father of George next-named, and of Richard 1636.

White, John, of Wilts, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. entry 17 Oct., 1600, aged 14; minister of Monkton Deverill, and vicar of Cheriton, now Chirton, near Devizes, 1615, until ejected, restored 1660, practiced physic at Conock; died at Chirton 6th, and buried there 8th Dec., 1671. See Ath. iii. 943; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Cartwright, John, of co. York, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 17 Oct., 1600, aged 13; sup. for B.A. 6 July, 1603, as 'arm. fil. n. m.,' from Queen's Coll., one of these names rector of West Itchenor, Sussex, 1616. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus. For another of these names see Ath., ii. 114.

Parker, Richard, of Kent, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 12 Dec., 1600, aged 17, B.A. 15 June, 1604; bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1614, treasurer 1641, as son and heir of Henry, late of Northfleet, Kent, gent., deceased. See Foster's Judges and Barristers; & Lansdowne MS. 984, f. 19.

Bishopp, Allen, of Dorset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 30 Jan., 1600-1, aged 18; B.A. 7 Feb., 1604-5, rector of Stalbridge 1609, vicar of Frampton-on-Severn, co. Gloucester, 1622, and of Ashton Keynes, Wilts, 1625. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Maplesden, Gervase, of Kent, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 12 Dec., 1600, aged 16; student of Middle Temple 1602, as son and heir of Edward, of Maidstone, Kent, gent; father of the next. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Pelland, John, of Sussex, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. entry 17 Oct., 1600, aged 13; perhaps B.A. from Magdalen Coll. 25 Jan., 1608-9, as Pellham.
Greene, Richard, of Middlesex, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 12 Dec., 1600, aged 16; B.A. 18 May, 1604, M.A. 30 Jan., 1606-7.
Woodhall, William, of Essex, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 17 Oct., 1600, aged 16.
Poore, Alexander, of Hants, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 17 Oct., 1600, aged 15.
Jeay, Thomas, of Dorset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 13 Feb., 1600-1, aged 16.

Capell, Richard (Caple), pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 19 June, 1601, aged 14; demy Magdalen Coll., 1604-8, B.A. 4 Feb., 1604-5, M.A. 14 Dec., 1607, fellow 1608-14 (son of Christopher, a stout alderman of the city of Gloucester), rector of Eastington, co. Gloucester, 1613-35, had licence from the Bishop of Gloucester to practice physic, and settled at Pitchcombe near Stroud, co. Gloucester, where he died 21 Sept., 1656, buried in the church, father of Christopher and Daniel 1643. See Bloxam, v. 22; Ath., iii. 421; D.N.B.; & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Jackson, Robert, of co. Durham, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 12 June, 1601, aged 16; B.A. 4 Feb., 1604-5, M.A. 20 Jan., 1607-8; one of these names knighted 13 May, 1617, alderman and M.P. Berwick-upon-Tweed 1621-2, 1624-5, 1625, 1626, Oct., 1628-9; buried 29 Jan., 1645. See Raine's North Durham, 180.

Highmore, Nathaniel, of Southants, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 16 Oct., 1601, aged 16; B.A. from Merton Coll. 27 Nov., 1604; M.A. from Queen's Coll. 17 June, 1607, rector of Goathill, Somerset, 1614, of Caundle Purse, Dorset, 1614. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Richards, Edward, of Southants, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 16 Oct., 1601, aged 18; probably son of Edward, of Yaverland, Isle of Wight, and father of Edward 1634, see also John 1632, 1635, and 1673. See Berry's Hants, 18.

Forde, James, of Somerset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 16 Oct., 1601, aged 17; B.A. 17 April, 1605, M.A. 20 Jan., 1607-8, rector of Hawkchurch, Dorset, 1607, and of Rampisham 1618. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Gore, Thomas, of co. Leicester, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 16 Oct., 1601, aged 16 (subs. "Goore"); B.A. 8 June, 1605, a student of Gray's Inn 1606, as of Lutterworth, co. Leicester. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Woode, John, of Kent, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 16 Oct., 1601, aged 17, B.A. 8 June, 1605; M.A. from Magdalen Coll. 8 June, 1608; vicar of Marden, Kent, 1614. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Barcham, Francis (Barcam), of Devon, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 16 Oct., 1601, aged 15; B.A. from Corpus Christi Coll. 20 June, 1605, M.A. 18 March, 1608-9, subscribed as Barcham.
Bird, Edward (Berde), of Somerset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 16 Oct., 1601, aged 16; B.A. from St. Mary Hall 17 June, 1605, M.A. 23 June, 1609, called Byrd in Mat. Reg.
Nicholls, "Surtonhil,", of Kent, cler. fil. St. Alban Hall, matric. 16 Oct., 1601, aged 16; B.A. from Corpus Christi Coll. 4 Feb., 1607-8.
Dickinson, Lewis (Dickenston), of co. Stafford, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 16 Oct., 1601, aged 17; called Dickinson in Mat. Reg.
Norden, Samuel, of Sussex, cler. fil. St. Alban Hall, matric. 16 Oct., 1601, aged 16; B.A. 4 Feb., 1604-5, M.A. 12 May, 1608.
Pittmann, Hugh, of Southants, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. entry 11 Dec., 1601, aged 14.
Toke, Nicholas (Tooke), of Kent, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 16 Oct., 1601, aged 17.
Lodge, Thomas, of Notts, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 16 Oct., 1601, aged 18.
Curtis, Nicholas, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 19 June, 1601, aged 17.

Trenchard, John, of Dorset, militis fil. St. Alban Hall matric. 15 Oct., 1602, aged 16, B.A. 18 Jan., 1604-5; of Warmwell, Dorset, student of Middle Temple 1606 (as 3s. George, of Charminster, Dorset, kt.), M.P. Wareham 1621-2, 1624-5, 1625, AprilMay 1640, 1640-53 (L.P.), Dorset 1654-5, 1656-8, Weymouth and Melcombe Regis 1659, a rumper, a commissioner on trial of the king, but did not act; brother of Thomas 1599, and father of John next-named. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Sherarde, (Sir) William, of co. Leicester, arm. fil. St. Alban Hall, matric. 29 Oct., 1602, aged 15; student of Gray's Inn 1604, as of Stapleford, co. Leicester (2s. Francis); gent. pensioner to James I., knighted 3 July, 1622, created Lord Sherard and Baron Le Trim, in Ireland, 10 July, 1627; died 16 April, 1640; brother of Philip 1597, and George 1602, and father of Bennet 1639. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Ashburnham, Edward, of Sussex, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 13 Nov., 1602, aged 13; B.A. 1 July, 1606, M.A. 13 July, 1611 (son of Adam), vicar of Tunbridge, Kent, 1617, rector Guestling, Sussex, 1632, preb. of Chichester 1642, brother of Charles, 1602, and John, 1598. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Sherarde, (Sir) George, of co. Leicester, arm. fil. St. Alban Hall, matric. 29 Oct., 1602, aged 16; student of Middle Temple 1604 (as 2s. Francis, of Stapleford, co. Leicester, esq.), died unmarried 1651; brother of Philip 1597, and of William 1602. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Massinger, Philip, of Salisbury, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 14 May, 1602, aged 18 (s. Arthur); poet and dramatist; died in his house on the Bankside in Southwark, buried in the Bull Head churchyard of St. Saviour's church 20 March, 1639. See Ath. ii. 654.

Nevinsonn, Richard, of Kent, arm. St. Alban Hall, matric. 18 June, 1602, aged 17, B.A. sup. 3 Dec., 1605; student of Middle Temple 1607 (as 3s. Thomas, of Eastry, esq., deceased); died in 1625, brother of Roger. See Foster's Inns of Court Register.

Petty, William, of Southants, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 14 May, 1602, aged 16; B.A. 8 June, 1605, M.A. 19 April, 1608, B.D. 10 March, 1620-1, vicar of Thorley, Isle of Wight, 1614; father of the next, etc. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Ashburnham, Charles, of Sussex, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 13 Nov., 1602, aged 15, B.A. 1 July, 1606; B.C.L. from All Souls' Coll. 21 Jan., 1612-13 (son of Adam), brother of Edward 1602, of John 1598, and of Laurence 1598.

Bentley, John, of co. Warwick, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 15 Oct., 1602, aged 15; B.A. from Merton Coll. 3 Dec., 1606, one of these names vicar of Croxall, co. Derby, 1617. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Berry, Richard, of co. Lincoln, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 15 Oct., 1602, aged 16; B.A. from Lincoln Coll. 2 July, 1606, M.A. 8 May, 1609, B.Med. (sup. 28 June) 1614, died in June, 1651. See Fasti, i. 357.
Powell, Richard, of co. Gloucester, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 23 April, 1602, aged 19; B.A. supd. 3 Dec., 1605, rector of Charfield, co. Gloucester, 1610. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Messinger, Philip (Massinger), of Salisbury, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 14 May, 1602, aged 18; poet and dramatist (s. Arthur); died 1639. See Bloxam, iv. 234.
Dowlinge, Samuel, of Wilts, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 14 May, 1602, aged 17; vicar of Sherston Magna, Wilts, 1612. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Starr, Samuel, of Dorset, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 13 Nov., 1602, aged 17; B.A. from Christ Church 18 June, 1607, M.A. 6 July, 1610.
Hughes, Stephen (Hewes), of co. Gloucester, arm. fil. nat. max. St. Alban Hall, matric. 15 Oct., 1602, aged 15, B.A. 8 June, 1605.
Herbert, John, of Berks, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 15 Oct., 1602, aged 15; B.A. 20 Oct., 1607, M.A. 6 July, 1612.
Fadis, John, of co. Hereford, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 15 Oct., 1602, aged 17; B.A. 21 Feb., 1605-6.
Greene, George, of Kent., gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 10 Dec., 1602, aged 17; B.A. 1 July, 1606.
Longe, Thomas, of Wilts, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 25 June, 1602, aged 15. See John 1605.
Yate, Thomas, of Berks, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 14 May, 1602, aged 16. See John 1596.
Brooke, Thomas, of Oxon, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 13 Nov., 1602, aged 16.
Whetcombe, John, of Kent, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 14 May, 1602, aged 18.

Verney, (Sir) Edmund, of Middx., militis fil. St. Alban Hall, matric. 9 March, 1603-4, aged 14; of Middle Claydon, Bucks, knighted 7 Jan., 1610, knight marshal and standard-bearer to Charles I. (s. Sir Edmund); M.P. Buckingham 1624-5, New Romney 1625, Aylesbury 1628-9, Chipping Wycombe April-May 1640, and 1640 (L.P.) till killed at the battle of Edge Hill 23 Oct., 1642; half-brother of Francis 1600, and father of Edmund next-named. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Dickinson, William, of Bucks, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 15 April, 1603, aged 18; fellow Merton Coll. 1607, B.A. 24 Nov., 1606, M.A. 9 March, 1611-12, B.D. 24 Nov., 1619, licenced to preach 21 July, 1625 (son of Thomas Dick, cook of Eton College), rector of Besselsleigh 1619, and of Appleton, Berks, 1620, father of Edmund 1642. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus; Ath., iv. 477; & O.H.S., iv. 278.

Dauntesey, Thomas, of Wilts, gent. St. Alban Hall matric. 9 March, 1603-4, aged 17; B.A. 26 Nov., 1606, of Lincoln's Inn 1608 (as son of Sir John, of Bishops Lavington, Wilts, knt.), brother of Ambrose 1594, and of William 1610 See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Bridges, Humphrey, of co. Gloucester, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 20 May, 1603, aged 14; student of the Middle Temple 1608, as son and heir of Humphrey, of Cirencester, co. Gloucester, gent., brother of Anthony 1612. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Lloyd, Thomas (Lhuide), of co. Gloucester, cler. fil. St. Alban Hall, matric. 2 March, 1603-4, aged 17; B.A. 6 May, 1607, M.A. 3 July, 1610, as Lloyd, then in orders; perhaps rector of Mongewell, Oxon, 1614. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Long, Kingsmell, of Southants, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 2 March, 1603-4, aged 15; student of Lincoln's Inn 1608, as of Somerset, gent. See William same date. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Savile, John, of Notts, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 8 July, 1603, aged 16; student of Middle Temple 1606, as son and heir of William, of (Oxton ?) Notts. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Longe, William, of Southants, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 2 March, 1603-4, aged 16. See Kingsmill, same date.
Raye, Robert (Raie), of Northants, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 8 July, 1603, aged 20.
Beere, Richard, of Somerset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 8 July, 1603, aged 18.

Thornburgh, Edward, of Middlesex, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 22 March, 1604-5, aged 18; B.A. from Merton Coll. 17 Dec., 1607; M.A. from St. Alban Hall 21 June, 1610 (as "epi. fil."), created D.D. from Christ Church 31 Aug., 1636 (s, John, bishop of Worcester), rector of Witchampton 1612, of Ower Moigne 1614, and of Turnerspuddle (all Dorset), 1615, archdeacon of Worcester 1629, died 1645; brother of John 1599. See Fasti, i. 495; Lansdowne MS. 985, f. 89; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Prigge, Richard, of Somerset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 12 Oct., 1604, aged 16; B.A. from Brasenose Coll. 2 May, 1608, M.A. 24 Jan., 1610-11, vicar of Kingsbury Episcopi 1610, and of Woolavington 1626, rector of Ilchester (all Somerset) 1637; father of Samuel 1634. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Style, (Sir) Thomas, of Middlesex, arm. fil. nat. max. St. Alban Hall, matric. 18 May, 1604, aged 17; student of Middle Temple 1606 (as o.s. Oliver, citizen of London), created a baronet 21 April, 1627, and died 18 Oct. following; father of Thomas 1641. See Foster's Baronetage.

Bate, Thomas, of co. Gloucester, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 22 March, 1604-5, aged 19; B.A. 20 Feb., 1608-9, bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1618, as son and heir of Richard, late of Newent, co. Gloucester, gent., deceased. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Baker, William, of co. Monmouth, arm. fil. nat. max. St. Alban Hall, matric. 20 April, 1604, aged 18; a student of the Middle Temple 1606, as son and heir of Richard, of Abergavenny, co. Monmouth, esq., deceased. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Fleetwood, George, of Middlesex, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 18 March, 1604-5, aged 18; perhaps youngest son of Sir William Fleetwood, of Cranford and Ealing, co. Middlesex, who was living 1625.
Whitfeld, Robert, of Oxon, cler. fil. St. Alban Hall, matric. 18 May, 1604, aged 16, B.A. 20 Oct., 1607, M.A. 9 May, 1611; rector of Liddiard Milicent, Wilts, 1614. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Hughes, William, of London, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 18 Jan., 1604-5, aged 17; student of Gray's Inn 1606, as son of Reginald, of city of London, gent. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Colthurst, Richard (Colthuste), of Middlesex, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 18 May, 1604, aged 17; B.A. 20 Oct., 1607.
Longe, John, of Wilts, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 8 March, 1604-5, aged 17; B.A. 15 Dec., 1608. See Thomas 1602.
Mascall, Richard, of Sussex, arm. fil. nat. max. St. Alban Hall, matric. 18 May, 1604, aged 17.
Turner, John, of Sussex, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 22 March, 1604-5, aged 16.
Fogge, Thomas, of Kent, arm. St. Alban Hall, matric. 12 Oct., 1604, aged 19.

Knight, Peter, of Kent, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 7 June, 1605, aged 16; B.A. from Magdalen Hall 19 June, 1609, M.A. 16 May, 1612, student of Gray's Inn 1607, late of Selling, Kent, gent.; vicar of New Romney, Kent, 1613. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg. & Foster's Index Eccl.

Locksmith, William, of co. Gloucester, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 29 Nov., 1605, aged 17; B.A. 20 Feb., 1608-9, M.A. 6 July, 1612, student of Gray's Inn 1616, as of city of Gloucester, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Fry, William, of Devon, arm. fil. nat. max. St. Alban Hall, matric. 3 May, 1605, aged 17; student of the Inner Temple 1609, as of Yarty, Devon (s. Nicholas); buried at Membury 1 May, 1672. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Rendall, George, of Somerset, cler. fil. St. Alban Hall, matric. entry 5 July, 1605, aged 14; B.A. from Merton Coll. 11 May, 1609; M.A. from St. Alban Hall 18 June, 1613, as Randall, which see, page 1231.

Arscott, Ezeckiel (Ayscott), of Devon, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 28 June, 1605, aged 15; B.A. 15 Dec., 1608, M.A. 7 July, 1615, of Lamerton, Devon, made his will 3 March, 1647, proved 22 March, 1653.
Hughes, Humphrey, of London, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 19 April, 1605, aged 15 student of Middle Temple 1608, as 2nd son of Reginald, of London, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Randall, George, of Somerset, cler. fil. St. Alban Hall, matric. entry 5 July, 1605, aged 14, as Rendall; B.A. from Merton Coll. 11 May, 1609, M.A. from St. Alban Hall 18 June, 1613.
Hodgkinson, Samuel, of Northants, cler. fil. St. Alban Hall, matric. 14 June, 1605, aged 19; vicar of Kingsbury, co. Warwick, 1616. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Lloid, Hugh, of. co. Radnor, minister. St. Alban Hall, matric. 10 May, 1605, aged 30; this man may be identical with either of the two preceding entries.
Norton, Richard, of co. Gloucester, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 28 June, 1605, aged 15; perhaps will at Oxford proved 24 Feb., 1630.
Knight, James, of Kent, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 7 June, 1605, aged 17.

Lenthall, William (Leynthall), of Oxon, arm. St. Alban Hall, matric. 23 Jan., 1606-7, aged 15; bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1616 (as son of William, of Wilcot, Oxon), bencher 1633, recorder of Woodstock and M.P. 1624-5, April-May 1640, 1640 (l.p.) —1653. Oxfordshire 1654-5, 1656-8, 'Lord' 1657, and a rumper 1659, recorder of Gloucester and Woodstock 1637, speaker 1640-53, and 1654-5, master of the rolls 1643-60, a commissioner of great seal 1646-8 and 1659-60; born at Henley-on-Thames June 1591, died 3 Sept., 1662; father of John 1640. See Ath. iii. 603; & Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Alchorn, Edward, of Kent, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 31 Oct., 1606, aged 18; (? B.A. from Clare Coll., Cambridge, 1616, and incorp. as M.A. 10 July, 1621) of Boughton Monchelsea, Kent, late of Clifford's Inn, admitted to Inner Temple in 1606. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Colepeper, William, of Kent, militis fil. St. Alban Hall, matric. 31 Oct., 1606, aged 18: B.A. 11 May, 1609, of Gray's Inn 1611, as son and heir of Thomas, of Aylesford, Kent, knight. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Wall, John, of co. Gloucester, cler. fil. St. Alban Hall, matric. 13 Feb., 1606-7, aged 17; one of these names rector of Burthorpe, co. Gloucester, 1622. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Percivall, John (Persivall), of Somerset, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 31 Oct., 1606, aged 15; probably son of James of Weston in Gordano. See Collinson's Somerset, iii. 175.
Bolde, William (Boulde), of co. Southampton, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 28 Nov., 1606, aged 17; student of Lincoln's Inn 1607. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Newton, Edward, of Somerset, arm. St. Alban Hall, matric. 23 Jan., 1606-7, aged 18, B.A. 15 Feb., 1609-10.
Baskervile, James, of co. Radnor, arm. St. Alban Hall, matric. 11 April, 1606, aged 21.

Selwyn, William, of co. Gloucester, arm. St. Alban Hall, matric. entry 5 June, 1607, aged 14; baptised 7 Oct., 1593; bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1618 (as son Jasper of Matson, co. Gloucester), died 1643; father of the next. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Roper, William (subscribes Rooper), of co. Hereford, militis fil. St. Alban Hall, matric. entry dated 5 June, 1607, aged 13; probably of Malmains, Kent, 2nd son of Sir Christopher Roper, afterwards 2nd baron Teynham. See Foster's Peerage.

N(o)urse, Walter, of co. Hereford, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 5 June, 1607, aged 16; B.A. from Broadgates Hall 8 July, 1612, bar.-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1619, as of Weston-under-Penniard, co. Hereford. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Eles, John, of Bucks, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 19 June, 1607, aged 15; demy Magdalen Coll. 1608-15, B.A. 10 June, 1611, M.A. 5 July, 1614, fellow 1615-18. See Bloxam, v. 31.
Waters, Adam, of Wilts, cler. fil. St. Alban Hall, matric. 4 Dec., 1607, aged 17; B.A. from Merton Coll. 11 July, 1611.

Petipher, Robert (Pettipher), of co. Gloucester, cler. fil. St. Alban Hall, matric. 3 Feb., 1608-9, aged 15; B.A. from Exeter Coll. 4 May, 1612, M.A. 16 Feb., 1614-15, vicar of Shottswell, co. Warwick, 1621, of Enstone, Oxon, 1627, and of Coleshill, co. Warwick, 1627. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Payne, Thomas, of co. Gloucester, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 3 Feb., 1608-9, aged 17, B.A. 6 July, 1612; one of these names vicar of Deverill Longbridge, Wilts, 1622, and father of Walter 1634, and of Samuel 1631. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Workman, John, of co. Gloucester, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 3 June, 1608, aged 18, B.A. 27 May, 1611, M.A. 8 July, 1614.
Ford, Richard (Foord), of co. Gloucester. pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 3 Feb., 1608-9, aged 18.
Dring, Simon, of Oxon, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 10 June, 1608, aged 18.

Perrot, Edward (Parret), of Oxon, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 24 March, 1609-10, aged 15, B.A. sup. 20 Feb., 1611-12; bar.-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1621, as of Northleigh, Oxon; born at Abingdon 7 Feb., 1593 (s. Robert 1571); died in Oxford 27 Feb., 1684, buried at Northleigh; father of Charles 1647, and of Robert 1639. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Newland, John, of Sussex, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 20 Oct., 1609, aged 23; B.A. 4 Feb., 1612-13, M.A. 7 July, 1615; curate of West Thurrock, Essex, licenced 5 May, 1621, to marry Mary Bromley, of the same. See Foster's Index Eccl. & London Marriage Licences, ed. Foster.
Moore, Andrew, of Somerset, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 24 March, 1609-10, aged 17; student of Middle Temple 1612, as 2s. Francis, of Taunton, Somerset, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Aynscombe, Henry (Ainscombe), of Sussex, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 8 Dec., 1609, aged 17, B.A. 8 June, 1613; fellow Merton Coll. 1613, M.A. 19 June, 1618. See O.H.S., iv. 280.
Nosse, Thomas, of Somerset, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 15 Dec., 1609, aged 16, B.A. 3 Nov., 1612.

Go(o)dwin, Hodges, of Somerset, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 18 June, 1610 aged 17; student of Inner Temple 1615, as of Bishop Hull, Somerset, gent.; another of these names bar.-at-law, Inner Temple, 1668. See Foster's Judges and Barristers; & Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Duncombe, John, of Bucks, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 18 June, 1610, aged 17; B.A. from Merton Coll. 1 March, 1613-14, of Gray's Inn 1614, as son of William, of Ivinghoe, Bucks, gent. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Dauntesey, William, of Wilts, militis fil. St. Alban Hall, matric. 18 June, 1610, aged 17; B.A. 17 Dec., 1611, M.A. 8 July, 1614, brother of Ambrose 1594, and Thomas 1604.
Ansell, William, of co. Gloucester. St. Alban Hall, matric. 22 Feb., 1610-11, aged 17; 'paup. schol.', B.A. 29 Oct., 1614, rector of Matson, co. Gloucester, 1626.
Slape, Richard, of Somerset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 18 June, 1610, aged 17.
Stydolfe, Thomas, of Surrey, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 16 June, 1610, aged 15.
Poulett, Hugh, of Devon, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 25 Jan., 1610-11, aged 17.

Robertes, John, of co. Gloucester, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 31 Jan., 1611-12, aged 18; perhaps B.A. from Christ Church 10 July, 1612, and student of Lincoln's Inn 1614, as son and heir of John, of Fiddington, co. Gloucester, who was M.P. Chippenham 1604-11. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Jones, Owen (Johnes), of co. Merioneth, pleb, St. Alban Hall, matric. 21 June, 1611, aged 18; perhaps B.A. from Merton Coll. 9 Feb., 1614-15, and M.A. from Hart Hall 8 July, 1619 (then in orders), and vicar of Machynlleth, co. Montgomery, 1626. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Browne, William, of co. Gloucester, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 31 Jan., 1611-12, aged 19; a student of the Middle Temple 1613, as son and heir of Henry, of Hasfield, co. Gloucester. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Warr, Amias, of Somerset, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 31 Jan., 1611-12, B.A. 7 July, 1615, M.A. 5 July, 1621; rector of Cheddon Fitzpaine, Somerset, 1620; father of Henry. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Parker, John, of co. Gloucester, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 31 Jan., 1611-12, aged 15; " paup. schol.," B.A. 7 July, 1615, rector of Alderton, co. Gloucester, 1628. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Browne, Thomas, of co. Gloucester, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 31 Jan., 1611-12, aged 18.

Blake, Robert, of Somerset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 26 Jan., 1615-16, aged 17; B.A. from Wadham Coll. 10 Feb., 1617-18 (son and heir of Humphrey, of Bridgwater), M.P. Bridgwater 1640, captain of dragoons in the parliamentary army, governor and defender of Taunton and a colonel, a commissioner of the navy, warden of the Cinque Ports, one of the 'admirals' or generals at sea 1649, a member of the Council of State 1651, won a victory at Santa Cruz 1657, etc., died 17 Aug., 1657, buried in Henry VII.'s Chapel, Westminster Abbey, but disinterred 12 Sept., 1661, brother of William 1620. See Fasti, i. 369; Gardiner, 47; & D.N.B.

Jackson, Thomas, of Kent, S.T.D. fil. St. Alban Hall, matric. 26 Jan., 1615-16, aged 18; B.A. 22 April, 1616, M.A. 4 Feb., 1618-19; incorporated at Cambridge 1627, rector of Great Chart 1617, of St. George, Canterbury, 1621, until sequestered 1645, vicar of Chilham 1624, and of Milton 1624, rector of Ivy church, 1629, all in Kent. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Hacket, Henry, of co. Gloucester, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 26 Jan., 1615-16, aged 19, B.A. 10 Feb., 1617-18; M.A. from Hart Hall 21 June, 1621, rector of Machen, co. Monmouth, 1620, vicar of Trelleck, co. Monmouth, 1625, rector of Ross, co. Hereford, 1627-42; brother of Robert 1617. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Emptage, Edward, of Kent, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 13 Oct., 1615, aged 15; B.A. from Corpus Christi Coll. 19 June, 1618; M.A. from St. Alban Hall 14 June, 1621, vicar of Postling, Kent, 1627. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Baseley, Edward, of Wilts, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 8 Dec., 1615, aged 19; a student of Gray's Inn 1618, as of East Knoyle, Wilts, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Eyres, Darius, of Wilts, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 3 Nov., 1615, aged 16 (called Ayres in Mat. Reg.); B.A. 19 May, 1617, M.A. 26 Jan., 1619-20.
Go(o)dwin, Geffery, of Wilts, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 13 Oct., 1615, aged 20; vicar of Froxfield, Wilts, 1628. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Martyn, Henry, of co. Warwick, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 3 Nov., 1615, aged 16; B.A. from Gloucester Hall 15 Dec., 1618, as Merton.
Colericke, Timothy, of co. Gloucester, ' cler. fil.' St. Alban Hall, matric. 13 Oct., 1615, aged 18.
Lilly, Nicholas (Lylley), of Dorset, cler. fil. St. Alban Hall, matric. 26 Jan., 1615-6, aged 17.
Dymocke, William, of co. Worcester, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 13 Oct., 1615, aged 17.
Walton, Samuel, of Northants, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 15 March, 1615-16, aged 19.
Bissell, Daniel, of Sussex, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 13 Oct., 1615, aged 16.
Hall, John, of Kent, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 1 Dec., 1615, aged 17.

Newsteade, Christopher, of co. Lincoln, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 22 Nov., 1616, aged 18; B.D. 21 April, 1631; (s. Thomas, of Somercotes, co. Lincoln), vicar of Abingdon St. Helen, Berks, 1629; licenced 3 Sept., 1631, to marry Mary, daughter of Antony Fulhurst, of Great Oxenden, Northants, clerk, deceased; rector of Stisted, Essex, 1642, until sequestered 1645 by the Westminster assembly, canon of St. Paul's 1660-2. See Ath. ii. 294; Add. MS. 15,669, p. 114; London Marriage Licences, ed. Foster; & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Collier, Edward (Collyer), of Wilts, 'cler. fil.' St. Alban Hall, matric. 16 Oct., 1616, aged 15; B.A. from Trinity Coll. 28 June, 1620, M.A. from Balliol Coll. 18 June, 1627, perhaps rector of Llampeter-Velfrey, co. Pembroke, 1636. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Lered, John, of Bucks, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 29 Nov., 1616, aged 18; B.A. 24 April, 1618, M.A. 29 Jan., 1620-1; buried in Merton college chapel 19 March, 1622-3, admon. at Oxford 3 May, 1623. See Gutch, i. app. 207.
Syndercombe, Gregory, of Somerset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 22 Nov., 1616, aged 20, B.A. 13 Oct., 1620; vicar of Nether-Stowey, Somerset, 1631. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Pill, Thomas, of Wilts, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 16 Oct., 1616, aged 15; perhaps vicar of Frampton-on-Severn, co. Gloucester, 1622. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Ditton, George, of Wilts, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 22 Nov., 1616, aged 19; vicar of Chitterne St. Mary, Wilts, 1629. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Messenger, William, of co, Gloucester, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 28 Feb., 1616-7, aged 17; B.A. 15 Dec., 1619.
Arnold, Nicholas, s. John, of Llantonie, co. Monmouth, arm. St. Alban Hall, matric. 29 Nov., 1616, aged 16.
Chambers, Humphrey, of co. Gloucester, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 8 Nov., 1616, aged 17.
Clifford, Anthony, of co. Gloucester, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 22 Nov., 1616, aged 17.
Wilsonn, Raphe, of Yorks, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 22 Nov., 1616, aged 16.

Bulstrod, Thomas, of Berks, arm. fil. nat. max. St. Alban Hall, matric. 28 Nov., 1617, aged 16; B.A. from Magdalen Hall 3 Feb., 1619-20, bar.-atlaw, Inner Temple, 1628, bencher 1646, a master in chancery 1657 until his death, son and heir of Henry, of Upton, Bucks, where he was buried 8 Feb., 1676-7. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Hackett, Robert, of co. Gloucester, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 5 Dec., 1617, aged 19; B.A. from Hart Hall 16 Nov., 1620; M.A. from Wadham Coll. 19 June, 1623; died of the plague, and was buried in the churchyard, July, 1625, rector of Mitcheltroy, co. Monmouth, 1622-5; brother of Henry 1616. See Gardiner, 65; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Harris, William, of London, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 28 Nov., 1617, aged 18; perhaps B.A. from Brasenose Coll. 3 May, 1621, M.A. 7 July, 1624, incorporated at Cambridge, 1637.
Nosse, Nathaniel, of Somerset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 28 Nov., 1617, aged 18; B.A. 27 Jan., 1620-1, M.A. 1 July, 1623.
Goulde, Benjamin, of Somerset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 28 Nov., 1617, aged 17; B.A. 9 May, 1621, M.A. 4 Feb., 1623-4.
Woodroffe, Thomas, of Wilts, cler fil. St. Alban Hall, matric. 28 Nov., 1617, aged 17.
Yardley, Edward, of Kent, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 28 Nov., 1617, aged 19.
Babham, Henry, of Bucks, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 28 Nov., 1617, aged 17.

Woodroffe, Thomas, of co. Gloucester, cler. fil. St. Alban Hall, matric. 13 Nov., 1618, aged 20, B.A. 23 Oct., 1620; M.A. from Balliol Coll. 25 June, 1623; rector of Gloucester St. Michael 1625, and perhaps rector of Chartham, Kent, 1646, until ejected 1662 for nonconformity, buried at Felstead, Essex, 29 Oct., 1689. See Foster's Index Eccl. & Calamy, iii. 324.

Cade, Thomas, of Somerset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 5 June, 1618, aged 18; B.A. 25 Oct., 1621, M.A. 2 June, 1624, vicar of Llanwenog, co. Cardigan, 1638, and rector of Trimley St. Mary, Suffolk, 1665. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Hinsley, Edmund, of Yorks, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 29 April, 1618, aged 18; B.A. from Merton Coll. 25 Feb., 1621-2, M.A. 10 July, 1624 (as "Hensley"), rector of Terrington, Yorks, 1625. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Drury, Robert, of Bucks, militis fil. St. Alban Hall, matric. 19 June, 1618, aged 15; B.A. 1 July, 1619, of the Inner Temple 1620, as 2nd son of Henry, of Hedgerley, Bucks, knt. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Laurence, Anthony, of co. Gloucester, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 12 June, 1618, aged 17; of Sevenhampton, co. Gloucester (s. Anthony), baptised there 1601, died 1643; brother of Francis same date.
Hensley, Edmund, of Yorks, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 29 April, 1618, aged 18, as "Hinsley"; B.A. from Merton Coll. 25 Feb., 1621-2, M.A. 10 July, 1624.
Imings, Richard, of Oxon, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 13 Nov., 1618, aged 16; of Stratford, Wilts, father of Richard, and Thomas next-named.
Laurence, Francis, of co. Gloucester, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 12 June, 1618, aged 14, B.A. 9 May, 1621; brother of Anthony 1618.
Fell, Henry, of London, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 5 June, 1618, aged 18; B.A. from Christ Church 26 June, 1620.
Harford, Emanuel, of Somerset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 5 June, 1618, aged 18; B.A. 17 July, 1620.
Allard, Richard, of Kent, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 5 June, 1618, aged 15; B.A. 23 Jan., 1621-2.
Michell, Richard, of co. Warwick, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 15 Oct., 1619, aged 20; one of these names rector of Rugby, co. Warwick, 1648. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Norborne, Henry, of Wilts, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 13 Oct., 1620, aged 19; B.A. 23 Oct., 1620, M.A. 1 July, 1623, B.D. 10 Feb., 1637-8; rector of Langley Burrell 1637, and of Yatesbury, Wilts, 1638, sequestered from Langley 1647, by the Westminster assembly. See Foster's Index Eccl. & Add. MS. 15,671, p. 81.

Bamfeild, James, s. (Amyas), of Devon, equitis. St. Alban Hall, matric. 19 Jan., 1620-1, aged 18; B.A. 9 May, 1621, M.A. 4 Feb., 1623-4, rector of Black Torrington, Devon, 1627, and of Rattery 1634. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Wookey, Nicholas, of Somerset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 19 June, 1620-1, aged 19, B.A. 17 Oct., 1621.

Cotton, Samuel, of co. Leicester, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 9 Nov., 1621, aged 18; B.A. 13 Feb., 1622-3, M.A. 2 May, 1626 (incorp. at Cambridge 1628), sup. for licence to preach 2 March, 1632-3, B.D. 13 April, 1633; D.D. from Pembroke Coll. 12 Sept., 1661, rector of Kilworth Abbas, co. Leicester, 1627, until sequestered in 1646, rector of Cotesbach 1634, the serjeant-at-arms of the House of Commons was ordered by the Westminster Assembly of Divines to bring him in safe custody before the commissioners on last day of Aug., 1647, canon of Lincoln 1660-5. See Add. MS., 15,671; & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Pavier, Tristram, of Somerset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 9 Nov., 1621, aged 19; B.A. 6 Feb., 1623-4; vicar of White Lackington, Somerset, 1642, sequestered to the rectory of South Warnborough, Hants, 1645, by the Westminster assembly. See Foster's Index Eccl. & Add. MS. 15,669, p. 225.

Noyes, William, of Wilts, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 16 Nov., 1621, aged 18; B.A. from Christ Church 13 Dec., 1624; M.A. from Corpus Christi Coll. 5 July, 1628, rector of Trimingham, Norfolk, 1637. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Gundrie, Hugh, of Dorset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 9 Nov., 1621, aged 18; B.A. 16 Feb., 1624-5, rector of Manston and of Maperton, Dorset, 1641, until ejected 1662. See Calamy, ii. 139; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Gibson, Thomas, of co. Warwick, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 16 Nov., 1621, aged 21; B.A. from St. Mary Hall 20 Feb., 1622-3, M.A. 17 April, 1627, rector of Baginton, co. Warwick, 1628. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Hallet, William, of Somerset, cler. fil. St. Alban Hall, matric. 9 Nov., 1621, aged 20; B.A. 13 Feb., 1622-3, vicar of Cutcombe, Somerset, 1624. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Bidgegood, George, of Somerset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 9 Nov., 1621, aged 21; B.A. 16 Feb., 1625-6 (called Bridgewood in Mat. Reg.).
Harris, Simon, of Bucks, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 14 Dec., 1621, aged 18; B.A. 4 Feb., 1623-4; M.A. from Oriel Coll. 14 Nov., 1626.
Saunders, Thomas, of co. Leicester, equitis fil. St. Alban Hall, matric. 13 April, 1621, aged 18; B.A. 9 May, 1621; son of Sir Matthew.
Baker, Edmund, of co. Somerset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 9 Nov., 1621, aged 20; B.A. 3 July, 1622, M.A. 23 June, 1625.
Hassoulde, Thomas, of Salop, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 9 Nov., 1621, aged 17; B.A. 26 Feb., 1624-5, M.A. 8 July, 1628.
Fishenden, George, of Kent, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 16 Nov., 1621, aged 19; B.A. 14 Dec., 1624, M.A. 30 June, 1627.
Burt, Ellis, of Dorset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 9 Nov., 1621, aged 19; B.A. from Magdalen Hall 16 Feb., 1624-5.
Browne, William, of co. Southampton, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 16 Nov., 1621, aged 15.
Hallett, John, of Somerset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 16 Nov., 1621, aged 20.
Poole, Walter, of Somerset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 9 Nov., 1621, aged 19.
Woodhowse, John, s. John, of Salwarpe, co. Worcester, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 21 June, 1622, aged 21, B.A. 3 July, 1622, M.A. 23 June, 1625.
Wilse, Thomas, s. John, of Dimmock, co. Gloucester, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 21 June, 1622, aged 21, B.A. 21 Feb., 1622-3.

Bab, Robert, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 4 Nov., 1623, aged 18; B.A. 7 Feb., 1623-4, rector of Heathfield, Somerset, 1628, vicar of Milverton 1629, rector of Exeter St. George 1633. See Foster's Index Ec.
Garth, Robert, s. George, of Morden, Surrey, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 14 Nov., 1623, aged 18; student of Lincoln's Inn 1623; brother of Anthony. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Martin, "Jeremie,", s. 'Jeronimi,' of Bath, Somerset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 14 Nov., 1623, aged 19; B.A. 8 Feb., 1625-6, M.A. (? restored) — Oct., 1630.
Knapp, Francis, s. Henry, of Chilton, Berks, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 14 Nov., 1623, aged 15; B.A. 2 June, 1627, baptised at Chilton 31 Dec., 1608.
Greene, William, s. John, of South Cadbury, Somerset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 20 June, 1623, aged 20; B.A. 29 June, 1623.

Welman, Thomas, s. Thomas, of Ilchester, Somerset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 22 Oct., 1624, aged 17, B.A. 23 Feb., 1627-8; vicar of Luppitt, Devon, 1639, until ejected 1662 for nonconformity, though he had also been compelled to desert his living in 1644-5; died in 1685; brother of the next. See Foster's Index Eccl. & Calamy, ii. 48.

Tayler, Robert, s. John, of Crewkerne, Somerset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 15 Oct., 1624, aged 18; B.A. from St. Mary Hall 4 Feb., 1625-6; M.A. from St. Alban Hall 21 June, 1628; one of these names proposed as minister for Glapthorne, Northants, 1647, by the Westminster assembly. See Add. MS. 15,671, p. 231.

Searle, John, s. Hugh, of Honiton, Devon, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 11 March, 1624-5, aged 20; B.A. from New Inn Hall 3 Feb., 1626-7; perhaps M.A. from Magdalen Coll. 21 June, 1634, and vicar of Rattery, Devon, 1656. See Foster's Index Eccl.; & Calamy, ii. 66.

Aylmer, Anthony (Aylemer), 3s. Samuel, of Akenham Hall, Essex, arm. St. Alban Hall, matric. 3 March, 1624-5, aged 16; bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1635, father of Brabazon Aylmer, a bookseller in London. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Thomas, George, s. Richard, of Tibberton, co. Hereford, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 22 Oct., 1624, aged 20, B.A. 18 Feb., 1625-6; rector of Thornbury 1640, and of Edwin Ralph, co. Hereford, 1641. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Farrar, George (Ferrar), 5s. Ambrose, of Newark, Notts, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 3 March, 1624-5, aged 19; one George Ferraro rector of Thornton Watlas, Yorks, 1635. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Bowne, John, s. John, of Middelzoy, Somerset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 22 Oct., 1624, aged 20; B.A. 9 Feb., 1625-6, one of these names vicar of Chewton Mendip, Somerset, 1662. See Weaver.
Eyre, Samuel, s. Thomas, of Bromham, Wilts, pleb. of St. Alban Hall, matric. 11 March, 1624-5, aged 20; B.A. 7 July, 1625, vicar of Hardenhuish, Wilts, 1643. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Beaumont, John (Beamount), s. John, of Wells, Somerset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 28 May, 1624, aged 22; B.A. from Corpus Christi Coll. 23 Feb., 1627-8.

Player, Thomas, s. Robert, of Canterbury, Kent, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 3 Feb., 1625-6, aged 17; B.A. 26 Jan., 1629-30, M.A. 11 April, 1633; perhaps rector of North Kilworth, co. Leicester, 1666. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Boden, Joseph, s. William, of Northwich, Cheshire, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 14 July, 1626, aged 20; one of these names minister of Ashford, Kent, in 1645 (Add. MS., 15,669, p. 182), and sequestered to rectory of Bath St. Peter. See Add. MS., 15,670, pp. 74, 170.

Cripps, John, s. Giles, of Clapton, co. Gloucester, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 19 Jan., 1626-7, aged 17; B.A. 26 Jan., 1629-30, M.A. 27 June, 1633, incorp. at Cambridge 1638, vicar of Turks Dean, co. Gloucester, 1633. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Barbour, John, s. Thomas, of Bockleton, co. Worcester, sacerd. St. Alban Hall, matric. 14 July, 1626, aged 16; B.A. 26 Nov., 1627, vicar of Eardisley, co. Hereford, 1642, and of Clifton-on-Teme, co. Worcester, 1662. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Wood, John, s. Richard, of Wiveliscombe, Somerset, sacerd. St. Alban Hall, matric. 14 July, 1626, aged 15, B.A. 6 Feb., 1629-30; perhaps rector of Wiveliscombe 1651; his father rector 1617, as Woods. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Barnes, Hannibal, s. Hannibal, of Ansty, Wilts, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 14 July, 1626, aged 20; B.A. 26 Jan., 1629-30, M.A. 20 Oct., 1632, rector of Oving, Bucks, 1660. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Stre(e)ter, Aaron, s. John, of Lewes, Sussex, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 14 July, 1626, aged 16; took holy orders, pretended to be a licenced physician of Oxford. See Ath. iii. 55.
Beach, Henry, s. George, of Salisbury, Wilts, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 19 Jan. 1626-7, aged 17; B.A. 6 Feb., 1629-30, M.A. 9 July, 1636.
Harries, Francis, s. Francis, of Abley, Salop, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 14 July, 1626, aged 16.
Hayne, John, s. John, of Exeter, Devon, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 1 Dec., 1626, aged 20.

Masters, James, s. John, of Englishcombe, Somerset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 4 May, 1627, aged 18; B.A. 18 Feb., 1627-8, M.A. 5 July, 1630, created B.D. 6 (or 17) June, 1646, rector of St. Peter and St. Paul, Bath, 1639, until sequestered in 1645 by the Westminster assembly, subdean of Wells 1661, rector of Farley Hungerford, Somerset, 1662; ? died 16 Aug., 1665; brother of Richard, father of Nathaniel. See Misc. Gen. et Her. n.s. iv. 71; & Add. MS. 15,669, pp. 181, 216.

Harison, (Sir) Richard, s. Richard, of Hurst, Berks, militis. St. Alban Hall, matric. 4 May, 1627, aged 14; B.A. 1 Feb., 1629-30, student of Lincoln's Inn 1631; knighted; suffered for the royal cause; gentleman of the bedchamber to Charles II.; died 23 Aug., 1683, aged 72; father of John 1659, William 1660, James 1668, and of Phelipps 1680. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Stephens, Nathaniel, s. Thomas, of Lipyate, co. Gloucester, arm. St. Alban Hall, matric. 4 May, 1627, aged 16; student of Middle Temple 1628, one of the commissioners for Gloucestershire 1654, for the ejection of "scandalous, ignorant, and insufficient ministers and schoolmasters." See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Haviland, Matthew s. Robert, of Hawksburye, co. Gloucester, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 25 May, 1627, aged 18; B.A. 26 Jan., 1629-30, M.A. 20 Oct., 1632; of Hawkesbury; died 1670, perhaps ejected from rectory of Holy Trinity the Less, London, 1662. See Calamy, i. 194; & Newcourt.

Nayler, Thomas (Nailer), s. John, of Clyffe, Yorks, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 4 May, 1627, aged 17; B.A. 14 Nov., 1630, M.A. 27 June, 1633, vicar of St. Nicholas, Newcastle-on-Tyne, 1663; buried in his church 15 April 1679; father of John 1672. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Meller, Henry, s. John, of North Lodge, co. Gloucester, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 4 May, 1627, aged 19; B.A. 20 Oct., 1630, M.A. 27 June, 1633 (as Miller); perhaps rector of Ryton by Beckbury, Salop, 1636, as Miller. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Harries, Stephen, s. Richard, of Cruckton, Salop, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 4 May, 1627, aged 14; B.A. 27 Jan., 1630-1, M.A. 19 Nov., 1633; brother of Thomas and of John 1627. S.H. of All Souls' Coll., M.A., admon. at Oxford, 3 Aug., 1640.

Clarke, William, s. Richard, of Walford, co. Hereford, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 22 June, 1627, aged 17; B.A. 26 Jan., 1629-30, M.A. 27 June, 1633, vicar of Dixton, co. Monmouth, 1635. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticu.

Bainbridge, John, s. John, 'doctoris.' St. Alban Hall, matric. 18 Feb., 1627-8, aged 16; B.A. 18 Feb., 1627-8, M.A. 3 June, 1630, one of these names vicar of Ashburnham, Sussex, 1632. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Coppin, Thomas, s. John, of Bekesbourne, Kent, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 4 May, 1627, aged 15; B.A. 22 Jan., 1628-9, M.A. 17 Oct., 1631, rector of St. Mary Bothaw, London, 1638. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Oglander, George, 2s. John, of Nunwell, Isle of Wight, Hants, militis. St. Alban Hall, matric. 4 May, 1627, aged 17; student of Middle Temple 1626; died 1632; brother of John 1633. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Harries, Thomas, s. Richard, of Cruckton, Salop, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 4 May, 1627, aged 15; bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1636; brother of Stephen and of John 1627. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Harries, John, s. Richard, of Cruckton, Salop, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 4 May, 1627, aged 16; bar.-at-law, of Gray's Inn, 1642; brother of Thomas and Stephen 1627. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Thomas, Marke, s. Alexander, of Lamberhurst, Kent, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 4 May, 1627, aged 18; student of Inner Temple 1631, and perhaps M.P. Rye 1659. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Jacob, Darcie, born in Kent, 3s. Abraham, of London, arm. St. Alban Hall, matric. 4 May, 1627, aged 14; B.A. 27 Jan., 1630-1, M.A. 19 Nov., 1633; brother of John 1617, and of Robert 1627.
Egleton, Christopher, s. Christopher, of Ellesborough, Bucks, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 4 May, 1627, aged 16; bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1636. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Hunt, Henry, 1s. James, of Popham, Hants, arm. St. Alban Hall, matric. 4 May, 1627, aged 16; student of Inner Temple 1627; brother of Richard 1632. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Blechinden, John, s. Thomas, of Bishopsborne, Kent, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 4 May, 1627, aged 15; B.A. from Magdalen Hall 1 Feb., 1630-1, brother of the last named.
Crossman, John (Crosman), s. Edmund, of Clere, Cornwall, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 4 May, 1627, aged 20; called Cresman in Mat. Reg.
Jacob, Robert, 2s. Abraham, of London, arm. St. Alban Hall, matric. 4 May, 1627, aged 16; brother of Darcy 1627, and of John 1617.
Uncles, Charles, s. Rodolphi, of Walford, co. Hereford, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 4 May, 1627, aged 18, B.C.L. 6 Nov., 1637.
Trevillian, Thomas, s. George, of Kingsbury, Somerset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 4 May, 1627, aged 20, B.A. 25 June, 1628.
Dawson, John, s. John, of Ockingham, Berks, gent. St. Alban Hall, matric. 4 May, 1627, aged 17; B.A. 26 Jan., 1629-30.
Trevillian, Richard, s. Rodolphi, of Drayton, Somerset, pleb. St. Alban Hall, matric. 4 May, 1627, aged 15.
